tasks.title: Create the /songs route and workspace shell
acceptance_criteria: The app exposes a /songs route inside the current Next.js structure.
The page provides a stable list-detail shell for scanning songs and reading one selected score.
The shell preserves context so the list remains visible while a score is open.
done_definition: A page-level container and workspace composition exist for the songs surface.

tasks.title: Support selection state and an explicit pre-selection empty state
acceptance_criteria: The list allows choosing a song and reflects the currently selected item.
The detail pane shows an empty state before the first song is selected.
Changing the selection updates the score viewer without leaving the page.
done_definition: Selection is managed by the workspace and the empty state is visible before selection.

epics.title: Deliver a clear score reading surface aligned with the design engine

tasks.title: Render song metadata and digitized section content
acceptance_criteria: The score viewer shows title, summary, and core attributes for the selected song.
Song sections render in a readable structure suitable for chord and notation text.
The UI optimizes for reading clarity over decorative presentation.
done_definition: Selected songs render complete detail content and maintain hierarchy across section blocks.

tasks.title: Apply the updated design rule engine to the songs workspace
acceptance_criteria: The page follows the list-detail workspace pattern captured in task memory.
The visual system uses a warm restrained editorial utility direction instead of cool grayscale defaults.
List and detail regions have clear hierarchy, quiet material treatment, and restrained emphasis.
done_definition: Page, list, and detail surfaces reflect the updated design engine constraints.

assumptions: Songs are sourced from local in-repo data for the MVP, so async loading and fetch retries are not required yet.
The songs page is an operator-style personal library surface, not a marketing or discovery page.
The current Tailwind styling layer remains the token source for this iteration.
risks: If the song dataset becomes remote or user-generated, additional loading and error states will be required in the detail pane and list shell.
A growing song catalog may require search, filtering, or virtualization beyond the current static list treatment.
Without screenshot-based review evidence, design review relies on code and class-level intent rather than rendered output.
